description.task: Declare variables of four different primitive data types in Java, assign them values, and print each variable with a label.
instructions: Declare an int variable named 'age' and assign it the value 20.
Declare a double variable named 'temperature' and assign it 36.6.
Declare a char variable named 'grade' and assign it 'A'.
Declare a boolean variable named 'isStudent' and assign it true.
Print each variable on a separate line in the format: 'age: 20', 'temperature: 36.6', 'grade: A', 'isStudent: true'.
description.hint: Remember: char literals use single quotes ('A') while String literals use double quotes. You can concatenate with + in System.out.println().
description.expectedOutput:
age: 20
temperature: 36.6
grade: A
isStudent: true

description.task: Demonstrate both implicit (widening) and explicit (narrowing) type casting in Java and print the results.
description.context: Type casting is the process of converting a value from one data type to another. Java handles some conversions automatically (implicit) and requires a manual cast for others (explicit).
instructions: Assign intVal (int) to implicitResult (double) directly — no cast needed.
Cast doubleVal (double) to int using (int) and assign to explicitResult.
Print all four lines in the exact format shown.
Note how the explicit cast truncates (not rounds) the decimal part.
description.hint: Explicit cast syntax: int result = (int) someDouble; — this drops the decimal part; 9.99 becomes 9.
description.expectedOutput:
int value: 42
double value: 9.99
Implicit (int to double): 42.0
Explicit (double to int): 9

description.task: Write a Java program that demonstrates what happens when an int variable exceeds its maximum value (integer overflow).
description.context: Every data type has a fixed range. Exceeding the maximum value causes it to wrap around to the minimum — this is called overflow and is a common source of bugs.
instructions: Run the starter code as-is — do not change it.
Observe the output and note that maxValue + 1 wraps around to Integer.MIN_VALUE.
Add a comment in your submission explaining why this happens.
Submit the code with your added explanation comment.
description.hint: Integer.MAX_VALUE is 2147483647 (2^31 - 1). Adding 1 causes a binary overflow and the value wraps to -2147483648 (Integer.MIN_VALUE).
description.expectedOutput:
Max int value: 2147483647
Max int + 1: -2147483648
Min int value: -2147483648
